# Promptise Foundry — Complete Reference

> Promptise Foundry is a Python framework for building production-grade AI agents. Five subsystems: Agent core, MCP Server SDK, Reasoning Engine, Agent Runtime, and Prompt Engineering. Install: `pip install promptise`. Python 3.10+.

## Quick Start

```python
import asyncio
from promptise import build_agent
from promptise.config import HTTPServerSpec

async def main():
    agent = await build_agent(
        model="openai:gpt-4o-mini",
        servers={
            "tools": HTTPServerSpec(url="http://localhost:8080/mcp", transport="sse"),
        },
        instructions="You are a helpful assistant.",
    )
    result = await agent.ainvoke({
        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello!"}]
    })
    for msg in reversed(result["messages"]):
        if getattr(msg, "type", "") == "ai" and msg.content:
            print(msg.content)
            break
    await agent.shutdown()

asyncio.run(main())
```

## build_agent() — Complete Signature

```python
async def build_agent(
    *,
    servers: Mapping[str, ServerSpec],          # REQUIRED — MCP server connections
    model: str | BaseChatModel,                  # REQUIRED — "openai:gpt-4o-mini", "anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-20250514", etc.
    instructions: str | None = None,             # System prompt
    memory: MemoryProvider | None = None,        # InMemoryProvider, ChromaProvider, Mem0Provider
    memory_auto_store: bool = False,             # Auto-store exchanges in memory
    conversation_store: ConversationStore | None = None,  # SQLiteConversationStore, PostgresConversationStore
    conversation_max_messages: int = 0,          # Max messages per session (0 = unlimited)
    guardrails: bool | SecurityConfig | None = None,     # True for defaults, or custom config
    cache: bool | SemanticCache | None = None,   # True for defaults, or SemanticCache instance
    optimize_tools: bool | OptimizationConfig | None = None,  # Semantic tool selection
    observe: bool | ObservabilityConfig | None = None,       # True for defaults, or custom config
    agent_pattern: str | PromptGraph | None = None,  # "react", "autonomous", "deliberate", "debate", "research", or PromptGraph
    node_pool: list[BaseNode] | None = None,     # Autonomous mode — agent assembles reasoning at runtime
    cross_agents: Mapping[str, CrossAgent] | None = None,    # Peer agents for delegation
    approval: ApprovalPolicy | None = None,      # Human-in-the-loop for sensitive tools
    events: EventNotifier | None = None,         # Webhook/callback event notifications
    sandbox: bool | dict | None = None,          # Docker sandbox for code execution
    extra_tools: list[BaseTool] | None = None,   # Additional tools beyond MCP-discovered
    flow: ConversationFlow | None = None,        # Prompt phase management
    adaptive: AdaptiveConfig | None = None,      # Failure classification and strategy learning
    max_agent_iterations: int = 25,              # Max reasoning steps per invocation
    max_invocation_time: float = 0,              # Timeout in seconds (0 = unlimited)
    trace_tools: bool = False,                   # Print tool calls to stdout
    graph_blocks: list[PromptBlock] | None = None,  # Custom prompt blocks for graph nodes
) -> PromptiseAgent
```

## Server Specifications

```python
from promptise.config import HTTPServerSpec, StdioServerSpec

# HTTP/SSE server (recommended for production)
servers = {
    "api": HTTPServerSpec(
        url="http://localhost:8080/mcp",
        transport="sse",                    # "sse", "http", "streamable-http"
        bearer_token="eyJhbGciOi...",       # JWT token forwarded to server
        api_key="sk-...",                   # Alternative: API key auth
        headers={"X-Custom": "value"},      # Custom headers
    ),
}

# Stdio server (local processes)
servers = {
    "local": StdioServerSpec(
        command="python",
        args=["-m", "mypackage.server"],
        env={"API_KEY": "..."},
        cwd="/path/to/dir",
    ),
}
```

## CallerContext — Multi-User Identity

```python
from promptise.agent import CallerContext

caller = CallerContext(
    user_id="alice",
    bearer_token="eyJhbGciOi...",   # Forwarded to MCP servers
    roles={"analyst", "admin"},
    scopes={"read", "write"},
    metadata={"ip": "192.168.1.1"},
)

# With ainvoke()
result = await agent.ainvoke({"messages": [...]}, caller=caller)

# With chat()
response = await agent.chat(message="...", session_id="session-001", caller=caller)
```

## Memory Providers

```python
from promptise.memory import InMemoryProvider, ChromaProvider

# In-memory (testing/prototyping)
memory = InMemoryProvider(max_entries=200)

# ChromaDB (persistent vector search)
memory = ChromaProvider(persist_directory="./memory", collection_name="agent_memory")

# Usage
agent = await build_agent(..., memory=memory, memory_auto_store=True)
```

## Conversation Stores

```python
from promptise.conversations import (
    InMemoryConversationStore,
    SQLiteConversationStore,
    PostgresConversationStore,
    RedisConversationStore,
)

store = SQLiteConversationStore("./conversations.db")
agent = await build_agent(..., conversation_store=store, conversation_max_messages=100)

# chat() auto-loads/saves conversation history
response = await agent.chat(message="Hello", session_id="session-001", caller=caller)
```

## MCP Server SDK

```python
from promptise.mcp.server import (
    MCPServer, MCPRouter,
    AuthMiddleware, JWTAuth, AsymmetricJWTAuth, APIKeyAuth,
    HasRole, HasAllRoles, RequireAuth,
    LoggingMiddleware, RateLimitMiddleware, TimeoutMiddleware,
    CircuitBreakerMiddleware, AuditMiddleware, ConcurrencyLimiter,
    MCPQueue, TestClient, HealthCheck,
)

server = MCPServer("my-api", version="1.0.0")

# Middleware stack
server.add_middleware(AuthMiddleware(JWTAuth(secret=os.environ["JWT_SECRET"])))
server.add_middleware(LoggingMiddleware())
server.add_middleware(RateLimitMiddleware(rate_per_minute=120))
server.add_middleware(TimeoutMiddleware(default_timeout=30.0))
server.add_middleware(CircuitBreakerMiddleware(failure_threshold=5))
server.add_middleware(AuditMiddleware(log_path="./audit.jsonl"))

# Routers for domain grouping
hr = MCPRouter(prefix="hr")

@hr.tool(roles=["admin"])
async def update_employee(emp_id: int, department: str) -> str:
    """Update an employee's department. Admin only."""
    return f"Updated {emp_id}"

server.include_router(hr)

# Background job queue
queue = MCPQueue(server, max_workers=4)

@queue.job(timeout=120, max_retries=3)
async def generate_report(dept: str, progress) -> str:
    """Long-running report with progress."""
    await progress.report(50, "Analyzing...")
    return "done"

# Testing (no network)
client = TestClient(server, meta={"authorization": "Bearer token"})
result = await client.call_tool("hr_update_employee", {"emp_id": 1, "department": "Eng"})

# Run
server.run(transport="sse")  # or "stdio", "http"
```

## Reasoning Engine — Node Types

### 10 Standard Nodes
- **PromptNode**: LLM reasoning with blocks, tools, strategy, model override, data flow, guards, pre/post processors
- **ToolNode**: Explicit tool execution with validation
- **RouterNode**: LLM-based routing to decide the next path
- **GuardNode**: Validate state and gate transitions
- **ParallelNode**: Run multiple nodes concurrently
- **LoopNode**: Repeat a subgraph until condition met
- **HumanNode**: Pause for human input/approval
- **TransformNode**: Pure data transformation (no LLM)
- **SubgraphNode**: Embed a complete sub-graph
- **AutonomousNode**: Agent assembles its own path from a node pool

### 10 Reasoning Nodes
- **ThinkNode**: Decompose a problem into sub-problems
- **PlanNode**: Create subgoals with quality threshold
- **ReflectNode**: Evaluate quality and decide to continue or loop
- **CritiqueNode**: Challenge assumptions, find gaps
- **SynthesizeNode**: Compose final answer from gathered context
- **ValidateNode**: Check criteria, route to pass/fail
- **ObserveNode**: Record tool results and state
- **JustifyNode**: Explain reasoning and cite evidence
- **RetryNode**: Retry with modified approach
- **FanOutNode**: Parallel execution of multiple sub-tasks

### PromptNode — Full Configuration

```python
from promptise.engine import PromptNode, NodeFlag
from promptise.prompts.strategies import chain_of_thought
from promptise.prompts.blocks import Identity, Rules

node = PromptNode(
    "analyze",
    instructions="Analyze the data thoroughly.",
    blocks=[Identity("Senior analyst"), Rules(["Cite sources", "Flag uncertainty"])],
    strategy=chain_of_thought,
    tools=my_tools,                    # Or inject_tools=True for MCP tools
    tool_choice="auto",                # "auto", "required", "none"
    model_override="openai:gpt-4o",    # Different model for this node
    temperature=0.0,
    max_tokens=8192,
    output_schema=AnalysisResult,      # Pydantic model for structured output
    guards=[SchemaStrictGuard(...)],
    input_keys=["search_results"],     # Read from state.context
    output_key="analysis",             # Write to state.context
    inherit_context_from="search",     # Inherit previous node's context
    preprocessor=my_preprocess_fn,     # async (state, config) → state
    postprocessor=my_postprocess_fn,   # async (output, state, config) → output
    include_observations=True,
    include_plan=True,
    include_reflections=True,
    flags={NodeFlag.RETRYABLE, NodeFlag.CACHEABLE, NodeFlag.OBSERVABLE},
    default_next="validate",
    max_iterations=5,
)
```

### Building Graphs

```python
from promptise.engine import PromptGraph, PromptNode, PromptGraphEngine

# Option 1: Autonomous — agent builds its own path
agent = await build_agent(
    model="openai:gpt-4o-mini",
    servers=servers,
    node_pool=[ThinkNode("think"), PlanNode("plan"), PromptNode("act", inject_tools=True),
               ReflectNode("reflect"), SynthesizeNode("synthesize")],
)

# Option 2: Named pattern
agent = await build_agent(..., agent_pattern="deliberate")
# Available: "react", "autonomous", "deliberate", "debate", "research", "pipeline", "peoatr"

# Option 3: Custom static graph
graph = PromptGraph("my-graph")
graph.add_node(PlanNode("plan", is_entry=True))
graph.add_node(PromptNode("execute", inject_tools=True, flags={NodeFlag.RETRYABLE}))
graph.add_node(ValidateNode("check", on_pass="synthesize", on_fail="execute"))
graph.add_node(SynthesizeNode("synthesize", is_terminal=True))
graph.set_entry("plan")
graph.add_edge("plan", "execute")
graph.add_edge("execute", "check")
agent = await build_agent(..., agent_pattern=graph)

# Option 4: Custom node with @node decorator
from promptise.engine import node

@node("verify")
async def verify(state):
    """Custom verification logic."""
    return {"verified": True}
```

### 16 Node Flags

ENTRY, TERMINAL, CRITICAL, RETRYABLE, CACHEABLE, SKIP_ON_ERROR, NO_HISTORY, ISOLATED_CONTEXT, LIGHTWEIGHT, VERBOSE, OBSERVABLE, INJECT_TOOLS, PARALLEL_TOOLS, AUTO_SCHEMA, MERGE_CONTEXT, STREAM.

## Agent Runtime

```python
from promptise.runtime import AgentRuntime, ProcessConfig, TriggerConfig
from promptise.runtime import BudgetConfig, HealthConfig, MissionConfig

async with AgentRuntime() as runtime:
    await runtime.add_process("my-agent", ProcessConfig(
        model="openai:gpt-4o-mini",
        instructions="Monitor and respond to events.",
        servers={"tools": {"url": "http://localhost:8080/mcp"}},
        triggers=[
            TriggerConfig(type="cron", cron_expression="*/5 * * * *"),
            TriggerConfig(type="webhook", webhook_path="/alerts"),
            TriggerConfig(type="event", event_pattern="orders.*"),
            TriggerConfig(type="file_watch", watch_path="./data", glob_pattern="*.csv"),
        ],
        budget=BudgetConfig(max_tool_calls_per_run=50, max_cost_per_day=10.0, on_exceeded="pause"),
        health=HealthConfig(detect_loops=True, detect_stuck=True, on_anomaly="escalate"),
        mission=MissionConfig(
            objective="Keep uptime above 99.9%",
            success_criteria="No P1 unresolved >15min",
            evaluate_every_n=10,
        ),
    ))
    await runtime.start_all()
```

## Prompt Engineering

```python
from promptise.prompts import prompt
from promptise.prompts.blocks import Identity, Rules, OutputFormat, ContextSlot, Examples
from promptise.prompts.strategies import chain_of_thought, self_critique, structured_reasoning
from promptise.prompts.guards import SchemaStrictGuard, ContentFilterGuard, LengthGuard
from promptise.prompts.flows import ConversationFlow, phase
from promptise.prompts.builder import PromptBuilder

# 8 block types with priority-based token budgeting
# When budget is tight, lowest-priority blocks drop first

# Conversation flows
class SupportFlow(ConversationFlow):
    @phase(initial=True)
    async def gather(self, turn): ...
    @phase
    async def diagnose(self, turn): ...
    @phase
    async def resolve(self, turn): ...

# Composable strategies
result = await (my_prompt
    .with_strategy(chain_of_thought + self_critique)
    .with_guard(SchemaStrictGuard(OutputModel, max_retries=3))
)(input_data)
```

## Event Notifications

```python
from promptise.events import EventNotifier, WebhookSink, CallbackSink

notifier = EventNotifier(sinks=[
    WebhookSink(url="https://hooks.slack.com/...", events=["invocation.error"]),
    CallbackSink(lambda event: print(event.event_type)),
])
agent = await build_agent(..., events=notifier)
```

## Installation

```bash
pip install promptise                    # Core (agent + MCP client + engine + runtime + prompts)
pip install promptise[ml]                # + ChromaDB, sentence-transformers, guardrails ML models
pip install promptise[infra]             # + Redis, Docker
pip install promptise[observability]     # + OpenTelemetry, Prometheus
pip install promptise[all]               # Everything
```
